title: Why Is Centene Corporation (CNC) Stock Down -10% Today?
author: tickeron_community
selftext: Centene Corporation ([CNC](https://tickeron.com/ticker/CNC/)) is one of the largest managed care organizations in the United States, providing government-sponsored health insurance through Medicaid, Medicare Advantage, and Affordable Care Act (ACA) marketplace plans to more than 28 million members. Shares plunged approximately 10% in Tuesday's session, falling from a prior close of roughly $43.16 to trade near $38.85, as the managed care sector faced a renewed wave of selling pressure. The move reflects deepening fears around federal healthcare funding policy, a structural reset in Medicare Advantage payment rates, and the potential erosion of Centene's core government-sponsored membership base.
